BusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: KAPPA KAPPA KAPPA INC GROUP RETURN

IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: THE OBJECT OF THIS ORGANIZATION SHALL BE TO BRING WOMEN INTO CLOSE, UNSELFISH RELATIONSHIP FOR THE PROMOTION OF CHARITY, CULTURE, AND EDUCATION.
IRS990.MissionDesc: THE OBJECT OF THIS ORGANIZATION SHALL BE TO BRING WOMEN INTO CLOSE, UNSELFISH RELATIONSHIP FOR THE PROMOTION OF CHARITY, CULTURE, AND EDUCATION.
IRS990.Desc: CONTRIBUTIONS FOR CHARITABLE, EDUCATIONAL, AND CULTURAL PURPOSES TO SUPPORT 501(C)(3) ORGANIZATIONS

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE D, PAGE 2, PART V, LINE 4
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: TO SUPPORT PHILANTHROPIC EFFORTS IN SUPPORT OF CHARITY, CULTURE AND EDUCATION IN OUR COMMUNITY.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE G, PAGE 3, PART III, LINE 17B
SCHEDULE I, PAGE 1, PART I, LINE 2
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: SCHOLARSHIP PAYMENTS ARE MADE DIRECTLY TO THE EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS. THE LOCAL CHAPTERS BUDGET FOR AND AWARD ALL CONTRIBUTIONS. THE CHAPTERS KEEP THE RECORDS (APPLICATIONS) FOR FOUR YEARS AFTER AWARDING. THE SELECTION CRITERIA IS IN THE SCHOLARSHIP GUIDE GIVEN TO ALL SCHOLARSHIP CHAIRS STATEWIDE AND IS ALSO AVAILABLE ON THE WEBSITE. THIS IS DISCUSSED DURING WORKSHOPS AND CONVENTIONS.

CHILDREN OF THE CHAPTER OFFICERS ARE ELIGIBLE TO RECEIVE SCHOLARSHIPS. SOME SCHOLARSHIPS ARE AUTOMATICALLY ISSUED TO THE SCHOOL'S VALEDICTORIAN AND SALUTATORIANS. EACH CHAPTER FOLLOWS THE GUIDELINES ISSUED BY THE STATE CHAPTER IN ISSUING SCHOLARSHIPS AND OTHER GRANTS.

LINE 2 - RELATED PARTY INFORMATION AMONG OFFICERS: THERE ARE SOME CHAPTERS WITH BOARD MEMBERS THAT MAY HAVE MOTHER-DAUGHTER, SISTER, OR OTHER FAMILY RELATIONSHIPS. IDENTIFICATION OF THESE RELATIONSHIPS WOULD BE AN UNDUE BURDEN ON THE GROUP FILING ENTITY.
THE ORGANIZATION IS ESTABLISHED AS A NOT-FOR-PROFIT CORPORATION. ALL MEMBERS HAVE THE RIGHT TO PARTICIPATE IN THE GOVERNANCE OF THE ORGANIZATION.

ALL MEMBERS OF THE LOCAL CHAPTERS HAVE EQUAL RIGHTS IN THE ELECTION OF THEIR ORGANIZATION'S GOVERNING BODY. ELECTIONS ARE HELD ON A REGULAR, PRE-DETERMINED BASIS AND ALSO AS UNEXPECTED VACANCIES OCCUR.
BY-LAW CHANGES ARE SUBJECT TO APPROVAL BY DELEGATES AT STATE CONVENTION.

EACH CHAPTER TREASURER PREPARES AN ANNUAL FINANCIAL REPORT FOR THE TAX YEAR. THIS IS REVIEWED BY THE CHAPTER AUDIT COMMITTEE. THESE REPORTS ARE SUBMITTED TO THE STATE TREASURER WHO REVIEWS THESE ALSO. THESE ARE THEN COMPILED TO FILE THE GROUP RETURN BY THE PAID PREPARER. THE STATE TREASURER THEN REVIEWS AND SIGNS THE 990 PRIOR TO FILING.

THE ORGANIZATION'S BY-LAWS ARE AVAILABLE ON THE WEBSITE FOR ANY MEMBER TO DOWNLOAD. FINANCIAL REVIEWS ARE PRINTED IN THE QUARTERLY MAGAZINE AND THE STATE CONVENTION REPORT. THESE ITEMS ARE ALSO AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST TO THE PUBLIC.
